name: SHM Position
whatItSolves: Gives the displacement of a simple harmonic oscillator at any time t: x(t) = A·cos(ωt + φ). The amplitude A and phase φ are determined from initial conditions x(0) and v(0).
whenToUse: Any undamped oscillator once ω, A, and φ are known. Use x(0) = A·cos φ and v(0) = −Aω·sin φ to extract A and φ from given initial values.
whenNotTo: Does not apply to damped oscillators — replace with x(t) = A·e^(−γt/2)·cos(ω_d·t + φ). Also invalid outside the linear (small-amplitude) regime.
commonMistakes: Assuming φ = 0 when the oscillator starts at maximum displacement but with a non-zero velocity. Mixing radians and degrees in ωt + φ. Using f (Hz) instead of ω = 2πf in the argument of cosine.
